title: helianthemum ocymoides subsp. algarvense
intro: Helianthemum ocymoides subsp. algarvense, also known as the Algarve Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cistus family, native to southern Portugal. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Algarve Rockrose is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and soothing properties.
